Between glazed and matte epoxy grout: matte epoxy grout hides water spots and mineral buildup better visually; gloss tends to highlight water spots, limescale, and soap residue due to reflections and contrast. Functionally both are non-porous epoxy and resist staining and moisture equally well; the difference is appearance. Cleaning: epoxy is easy to clean in either finish; glossy grout is marginally easier to wipe completely clean because it’s smoother, while matte can feel slightly more textured and may show residue up close. Color matters more than sheen: very dark grout shows white mineral deposits strongly; very light grout shows soap scum/dirt; mid-tones or a color that masks typical deposits in your water are a smart choice. Prevention: squeegee after showers and consider a water softener or point‑of‑use measures to reduce scale buildup. Installation/maintenance: use a quality epoxy specified for showers, and avoid abrasive pads on matte surfaces to prevent altering the finish. Bottom line: pick matte epoxy if your primary goal is to make mineral spots and water marks less visible; keep a good cleaning routine and choose a grout color that minimizes contrast with expected deposits.

Quick, non-invasive fixes to hide exposed plumbing under a kitchen sink when replacing the cabinet isn’t an option:

1) Tension‑rod sink skirt
- How: mount a small tension rod under the countertop and hang a curtain/skirt clipped on.
- Pros: very quick, no tools or adhesives, easy removal for access, inexpensive.
- Cons: fabric can collect dust/moisture; use mildew‑resistant or washable fabric.
- Low‑odor tip: use natural cotton/linen or pre‑washed fabric; avoid heavy PVC backings if someone is chemically sensitive.

2) Magnetic or clip‑on panel/skirt (if cabinet face is metal or you can attach a slim metal strip)
- How: attach a thin magnetic strip to the cabinet interior with removable adhesive, then use a magnetic panel or skirt with sewn magnets.
- Pros: tidy, removable, no permanent fasteners.
- Cons: needs a magnetic surface or a metal strip installed (use low‑VOC removable adhesive).

3) Removable front panel on Velcro / Command strips
- How: cut a lightweight plywood or rigid PVC panel
